growth_records.medium: Mineral salts medium (MSM) with 0.6 mM dipicolinic acid (DPA) as sole carbon source

up.method: HPLC time-course of DPA depletion in MSM; 0.6 mM fully removed within 8 h of active degradation after a 12 h lag phase. Rate = 0.6 mM / 8 h = 0.075 mM/h (0.075 mmol/L/h). Not biomass-normalized (no gDW reported).

growth_records.cite: Jiang, Yinhu et al. DipR, a GntR/FadR-family transcriptional repressor: regulatory mechanism and widespread distribution of the dip cluster for dipicolinic acid catabolism in bacteria. Nucleic Acids Research (2024). DOI:10.1093/nar/gkae728. [PMC11472048]
growth_records.method: not reported (cell growth measured by OD but no numeric mu given; only DPA substrate depletion quantified)
growth_records.snippet: wild-type strain JQ135 started to degrade DPA after a 12 h lag phase and completely removed the added 0.6 mM DPA within 8 h ... cells were further cultivated in 100 ml sterilized MSM containing 0.6 mM DPA on a rotary shaker (30 C, 180 rpm)
growth_records.notes: Substrate uptake computed from explicit paper numbers: 0.6 mM DPA consumed within 8 h => 0.6/8 = 0.075 mM/h. 12 h lag before onset (not counted in active rate). No biomass (gDW) so cannot convert to mmol/gDW/h; no growth rate or yield reported. T=30 C, 180 rpm shaking, MSM per ref 23 (full composition not stated in this paper). Organism Alcaligenes faecalis JQ135 (Betaproteobacteria). Regulation/genetics paper, not a GEM/MFA study.
